name: ICE/TIDE Result Interpreter
description: Translates raw ICE (Synthego) or TIDE (Brinkman et al. 2014) output into a plain-English interpretation with actionable next steps. Classifies editing efficiency (FAILED/MARGINAL/GOOD/EXCELLENT) and fit quality (HIGH/MODERATE/LOW), flags unreliable results when R^2 < 0.80, and analyzes the indel distribution to detect background-noise artifacts. Closes the loop on the verify_edit protocol — verify_edit tells the user how to set up sequencing, this tool tells them what the results mean.

inputs.name: editing_pct
inputs.description: Percent of reads with indels reported by ICE (KO score) or TIDE (total efficiency). Range 0-100.

inputs.name: r_squared
inputs.description: R^2 of the indel deconvolution fit. Range 0-1. Below 0.80 the result is considered unreliable.

inputs.name: tool
inputs.description: Which tool the result came from: 'ice' (default) or 'tide'. Affects naming in the report.

inputs.name: indel_distribution
inputs.description: Optional dict mapping indel size (e.g. '+1', '-3', '0' for unedited) to its percentage. Enables dominant-indel reporting and background-noise warnings.

inputs.name: sample_id
inputs.description: Optional sample label included in the summary.

outputs.description: A dict with efficiency_classification, fit_quality, is_reliable, dominant indel info, list of warnings, list of next_steps, and a plain-English summary.
